rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/8/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/25/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OMAR ABDULLAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/08/2021 again for same issues. No filed response. 1) Please explain why the attorney requests reimbursement of a $540.00 filing fee for General Conservatorship Petition on page 6 line 1 of the petition when the filing fee paid was $450.00. 2) Please file conservator timesheets that conform to LRSF 14.53. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. 3) Please explain and provide legal basis for why the conservator is requesting fees for tolls and mileage when LRSF 14.58 says tolls and mileage are absobed as part of fees. It appears this needs to be amended to remove the request for tolls and mileage. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/25/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRYETTE B. NESTEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) 05/18/2021 Nancy Y. Lewellen filed a Declaration Objecting to the Petition. Examiner note said, "filing Objections as a Declaration is not proper procedure. Nancy Lewellen must file proper Objections and pay a filing fee." No proper objections filed by Nancy Lewellen and no filing fee paid. 07/22/2021 William C. Nestel filed Objections and paid the filing fee. Objections state the re-filing of this petition is a breach of the Settlement Agreement and Release. 2) Examiner note, "please explain why the petition requests both the 7/2/2018 and 7/1/2019 Will be admitted when the 2019 Will says, "I hereby revoke all my previous Wills and codicils." No filed response. 3) Examiner note, "please explain and provide legal authority for why Trisha Friedeberg acting as personal representative is not a conflict of interest when it appears she also represents Matthew Nestel." No filed response. 4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] 5) Please explain why petitioner alleges the Will waives bond when the Will does not waive bond for Trisha Friedeberg. 6) Please explain how Trisha Friedeberg has priority for appointment under Probate Code 8461. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Intervivos Trust To Establish Trustee'S Claim Of Ownership Over Real Property And Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HERBERT D. CROSS AND ALICE CROSS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Compelling Trustees To Provide Information And An Accounting, Removing Trustees And Appointing New Trustee, And Remedying Breach
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Venue is a threshold issue that will be determined prior to the petition. a. Petitioner alleges venue is San Francisco. b. Dinell Cross alleges venue is in Sacramento County because Laron Cross abdicated his role and Probate Code 17002(b)(1) should apply. c. Laron Cross alleges the principal place of admin is in Sacramento County 2) Petitioner requests: a. Co-trustee be removed, denied compensation, their share be surcharged for losses caused by their actions and inactions b. Petitioner be appointed successor trustee. c. Co-trustees be assessed the attorney fees and costs incurred by Petitioner.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RALPH R. OROQUITA REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment And Damage & Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: continued to november 22, 2021. the petition was continued from its original hearing date as notice was mailed only 29 days prior to the hearing, (new notice was required). the new notice was mailed september 29, 2021 for a hearing on october 13, 2021, only 14 days. notice must be properly mailed no less than 30 days before november 22, 2021, and the notice of hearing form must show the continued hearing date. POS of the NOH must be filed by noon on novwember 15, 2021. in addition, by that same deadline, petitioner must file a supplement that shows the family tree, including the Trustor's siblings and their issue, with dates of death if appropriate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA G. VELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/15/2021 1) Please address LRSF 14.16 F. 2) Please address LRSF 14.18 C. 1) Artemis Millan is listed in Decedent's Will and also did not receive notice (Probate Code 1202.) 3) Please address LRSF 14.18 C. 2) 4) Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147) needs to be filed. 5) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/1/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA J. WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/8/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/25/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDIA M MARKEVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: continued to november 8, 2021. no proof of publication on file as of friday morning, 10/8/21 at 10:30 a.m., no confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form on file yet (notwithstanding reference to that document in the cover letter with other courtesy copies). deadline for getting both documents on file is noon on Monday, 11/1/21. please note, the confidential attachment must be filed in person, by mail, or by drop box. it cannot be efiled. if the documents are filed on time, an appearance will be required on 11/8/21 as petitioner lives out of state and is seeking appointment in pro per with full authority and minimum bond.
